ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific node for 3-in-1 flavoured instant tea; closest match is instant tea beverage (FOODON_03301099).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKM covers tea-based beverages broadly; no specific node for premixed 3-in-1 instant tea with creamer and sugar.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for 3-in-1 instant flavoured tea; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

allergens: milk derivatives (non-dairy creamer may contain milk proteins)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard sachet (approximately 20 g of powder dissolved in 150–180 ml hot water) represents a typical single serving of 3-in-1 flavoured tea.

nutrient_source.title: 3-in-1 Flavoured Tea — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 3-in-1 flavoured tea (cr_id D09020003). Values represent per-100 g of powder as consumed.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 3-in-1 flavoured instant tea. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the GI of sucrose (~65, University of Sydney GI Database) as the dominant glycaemic driver (5.67 g sugar/100 g, predominantly sucrose from added sugar). The non-dairy creamer and tea solids contribute negligible additional glycaemic load. GL per serving calculated as: GI × available carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 65 × 1.26 g / 100 ≈ 0.8. Serving size used: 20 g powder (2.52 g available carbohydrate per 20 g serving after accounting for 0 g fibre).

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar (5.67 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage format independently raises blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines, despite low sodium content (37 mg/100 g).
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid sugar-sweetened instant tea mixes; plain unsweetened brewed tea is a suitable low-sugar, low-sodium alternative.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 5.67 g sugar/100 g and zero dietary fibre; liquid sugar causes rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation with no fibre-mediated attenuation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this product as it is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage; ADA guidelines recommend eliminating SSBs and substituting plain unsweetened tea or water.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with elevated triglycerides, increased CVD risk, and weight gain; non-dairy creamer may contain partially hydrogenated oils contributing trans fat.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit sugar-sweetened instant tea mixes and choose plain unsweetened tea to avoid added sugar and potential trans fat from non-dairy creamer.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 3-in-1 Flavoured Tea is an ultra-processed instant beverage product widely consumed in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. It consists of tea powder premixed with non-dairy creamer, sugar, and additional flavourings, packaged in individual sachets. Preparation requires only dissolving the sachet contents in hot water, making it a convenient on-the-go or office beverage. The flavour profile is sweet, mildly milky, and lightly tea-forward, with the dominant taste coming from added sugar and creamer rather than pure tea. Per 100 g of powder, it provides 45 kcal, 1.02 g protein, 1.17 g fat, and 6.3 g carbohydrate of which 5.67 g is sugar. Dietary fibre is absent (0 g), and sodium is low at 37 mg/100 g. Clinically, this product is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) due to its sugar content exceeding 5 g/100 g in liquid form. It is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its added sugar load, absence of fibre, and potential trans fat contribution from non-dairy creamer. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium) driven by sucrose as the primary glycaemic substrate. Plain unsweetened brewed tea is recommended as a healthier alternative for all three conditions.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a sachet of 3-in-1 tea?
Is instant tea with creamer and sugar bad for blood pressure?
What is the sugar content of 3-in-1 flavoured tea?
Is 3-in-1 tea suitable for weight loss?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09020003, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. All core macros are present and internally consistent. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using sucrose as the primary glycaemic driver, clearly documented.
notes: dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source — this is consistent with an ultra-processed instant tea powder formulation containing no fibre-contributing ingredients. calcium_mg and iron_mg are reported as 0.0 per HPB source; these are confirmed zero values from the approved source. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, potassium_mg, and all micronutrient fields set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, cholesterol, and micronutrient data for this instant tea mix are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry as of 2026-04-16. water_g set to -1: moisture content of the dry powder is not reported in the HPB source. GI is an analogous estimate (GI 65, medium) based on sucrose as the dominant glycaemic substrate per University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in approved sources. Non-dairy creamer ingredient may contain milk-derived proteins; product is not suitable for strict vegans or those with dairy allergies without label verification. Halal status assumed based on typical Singapore market formulations but should be verified against product label certification.
